Mobile Options
This is a responsive theme, mobile options do not apply.
Remove Weebly branding and customize the footer on your website by subscribing to a plan.
Custom Theme file format details:
  • A zip file named [your theme name].zip
  • Template layout data in a file called index.html
  • Style information in a file called main_style.css
  • All image files associated with your theme
  • All files must be in the root directory of your zip archive. Any files in a subdirectory will not be imported

Checking
Domain is available!
Domain is not available
To set up your domain with Weebly, your domain's DNS settings need to be updated.
Option A: Email instructions to your domain registrar
Send these instructions to your domain registrar (ex. GoDaddy, 1and1, Yahoo, etc.)
Option B: Make the DNS changes yourself see instructions hide instructions
We have specific instructions for Google, GoDaddy, Yahoo, Wordpress, Namesilo, and 1and1.com.
For all other registrars, follow these general instructions:
  1. Log in to your domain's control panel
  2. Locate the area to change your domain's DNS settings
  3. Remove any AAAA DNS records for the domain.
  4. Create 2 A-Records to point to Weebly's IP address
A-Record Host Points to
#1 The root of your domain (usually identified by a @ or * symbol) 199.34.228.43
#2 www 199.34.228.43
If you experience any problems, email your domain registrar the instructions from Option A above. Unfortunately, we have no control over your domain's settings and cannot provide assistance.
Continue
Once the DNS changes are made, it may take up to 48 hours (although usually less) for the changes to propagate through the Internet
Your client's domain DNS settings need to be updated to point to our servers.
Option A: Email instructions to the domain registrar
Send these instructions to the domain registrar (ex. GoDaddy, 1and1, Yahoo, etc.)
Option B: Make the DNS changes yourself see instructions hide instructions
We have specific instructions for Google, GoDaddy, Yahoo, Wordpress, Namesilo, and 1and1.com.
For all other registrars, follow these general instructions:
  1. Log in to the domain control panel
  2. Locate the area to change the domain's DNS settings
  3. Remove any AAAA DNS records for the domain.
  4. Create 2 A-Records to point to Weebly's IP address
A-Record Host Points to
#1 The root of the domain (usually identified by a @ or * symbol) 199.34.229.100
#2 www 199.34.229.100
If you experience any problems, email the domain registrar the instructions from Option A above. Unfortunately, we have no control over the domain's settings and cannot provide assistance.
Continue
Once the DNS changes are made, it may take up to 48 hours (although usually less) for the changes to propagate through the Internet
You've successfully registered
Your domain will be active within a matter of minutes or hours (up to 4 hours).

  • Your confirmation id is .

  • You will receive a confirmation email with the total charged to your account.

  • Once you have received this email, your domain should be active.

  • In order to access your domain as soon as possible, do not visit your domain until you receive your email confirmation.

  • If your domain is not active within 4 hours, please contact support@weebly.com

GenBook Setup

You're setting up a Genbook account. You'll be able to accept online appointments through your website.

BookFresh Setup

Through a partnership with BookFresh, Weebly is able to offer your customers the ability to book appointments online. BookFresh puts your business' open time slots on the web and attracts new customers to schedule with you.

If you already have an BookFresh account, please use the same email address you used to sign up. New users will receive an email from BookFresh with account setup instructions.

Online Store Setup

To set up your online store, you'll need a PayPal Business/Premier account.

What type of page are you adding?
Standard Page (tall header image)
This header type has a tall header image with space for content below.
Standard Page (short header image)
This header type has a short header image with space for content below.
Standard Page (no header image)
This header type has a large space for content and no header image.
Landing Page
A landing page includes a call-to-action area with a picture, text, and button placed above the main content. It is often used for the website home page.
Splash Page
A splash page focuses your attention to the center of the screen. It's often used as an intro page, coming soon page, or sign up page for an email list.
Other
None of the above.
Continue

The video you're trying to upload is larger than the 1GB size limit.

When properly compressed, 1GB files should allow 30 minutes or more of HD quality video playback.

Please ensure your video is properly compressed by reading our guide.
